title: data/arcs.json (v1) and data/arcs-v2.json (v2) Share No Runtime Path — v1 Is Frozen, Not a File to Keep in Sync
problem: Before building any arc-membership automation for the v2 pipeline (a vex:arcs meta-tag / build-time slug-insertion feature, requested Session 022), Victor raised a real, previously-unresolved concern: does data/arcs.json (which the still-live Squarespace site fetches at runtime) depend on or get affected by anything done to data/arcs-v2.json (the v2 GitHub Pages build pipeline's source)? Neither party had full recall of whether that dependency existed.
exploration: traced every consumer of both files: data/arcs.json is read only by lib/vextreme.js, lib/archive-renderer.js, and lib/arc-nav.js (the v1 runtime path serving vextreme24.com directly via fetch) — none of lib/build-*.js ever reads it

traced data/arcs-v2.json's consumers: lib/build-index.js, lib/build-archives.js, lib/build-demo.js, lib/build-index-page.js, lib/check-key-alignment.js — all build-time only, output baked into data/index.json/dist/pages already served statically

found the two files use different per-arc schemas (v1: sections[].entries[] with {n,title,slug}, carrying its own titles; v2: sections[].slugs[], titles from nodes.json) but diffed identically on arc keys and slug membership — a snake_case-vs-kebab-case comment in lib/build-archives.js's ARC_KEY_MAP claiming arcs.json used kebab-case turned out to be wrong; both files use snake_case, and kebab-case is only the i18n string-key naming convention

diffed every arc's explicit slug set between the two files programmatically: zero drift on all 15 named/explicit-order arcs (full_timeline expectedly differs — chronological/computed in v2, hand-listed in v1)
decision: Confirmed directly with Victor: v1 receives no new Squarespace content going forward — it is deprecated in favor of v2, and the parity that currently holds between the two files is a frozen snapshot from before/around the v1→v2 split, not something anything actively maintains. No automated v1/v2 parity check was built, since Victor confirmed there's nothing left to drift against. data/arcs.json should be treated as read-only historical state for the live v1 site, not a file future arc-editing work needs to touch or keep in sync.
lesson: Two files that look like versioned copies of the same concept (same arc keys, same apparent purpose) can share zero runtime path — confirm the actual consumer set before assuming a dependency exists in either direction. The risk here wasn't 'v2 changes could break v1' (they can't — nothing during a v2 build ever touches data/arcs.json) or 'v1 changes could break v2' (same reason) — the real, subtler risk was that new arc-insertion tooling built for v2 only would silently drift from v1's current parity going forward, if v1 were still an active target. Once confirmed it isn't, that risk doesn't apply, but it would apply again the moment either file resumes taking new content — worth re-checking if that ever changes.
impact: No code behavior change. Fixed one stale/incorrect code comment (lib/build-archives.js's ARC_KEY_MAP). Documented the full v1/v2 relationship in docs/architecture/03-data.md so it doesn't need re-tracing. Unblocks building vex:arcs-style arc-insertion automation against data/arcs-v2.json alone, without needing to also write data/arcs.json or build a parity check for it.
